notes: Table 5: Ipinda 85 day-0 samples sequenced (pfk13 codons ~440-600). C469C synonymous mutation in 2 samples (nucleotide-level, amino acid remains C=wild type). All 85 carry wild-type amino acid C at 469. Reported nonsynonymous mutations at this site were at non-target codons only, so 469 wild type = 85. Reporting the 2 with C469C nucleotide change conservatively as still C amino acid; encoding all 85 as C469C wild-type amino acid. variant_num set to 85 minus none since amino acid is wild type; using 85.
Table 5: Nagaga 88 day-0 samples. C469C is synonymous (amino acid wild type C). All 88 wild type at codon 469 amino acid level.
Table 5: Karume 79 day-0 samples, 0 SNPs detected. All wild type C at codon 469 (range 440-600 covered).
Table 5: Simbo 94 day-0 samples; only P417P (non-target). All wild type C at codon 469.

Table 4: pfmdr1 N86 detected in all 328 successfully sequenced day-0 samples (100% wild type N86). Pooled across all four sites; cannot disaggregate. R0 (recurrent infection) samples 43/43 wild type not extracted separately to avoid conflating baseline population.
